column1: DivX News: DivX Affiliate Program, Nobody in DivX, and New Player Skins,"DivX News (Number 21, 2002-07-26)=================================What's Hot in the World of DivX(tm) Video This Week:DivX Affiliate Program, Nobody in DivX, and New Player SkinsNow Sent to Over 1.6 Million DivX Fans Worldwide! Welcome to this week's edition of DivX News, your weekly fix of information about what's hot and what's cool in the world of DivX video. (You are being sent this email because you subscribed to this newsletter at DivX.com. If you'd like to unsubscribe, follow the instructions at the end of this message.) In This Week's Issue -------------------- 1. Sell DivX Pro From Your Web Site 2. Japanese Crime Noir in DivX Video 3. Mad Phat Crazy Skins Sell DivX Pro From Your Web Site ------------------------------- DivX has become the world's most popular video technology and garnered millions of loyal fans thanks almost entirely to word-of-mouth buzz from users like you. We've always known that DivX users are the generous sort--the kind of people who want to share great-looking video technology with the rest of the world. But what if you could exercise your generous nature by spreading the DivX gospel while making money at the same time? Now you can. The DivX Affiliate Program allows web site owners to make money while providing users with the best video technology on the Internet. If you have your own web site, all you have to do is sign up to become an authorized DivX Pro Affiliate. Once you're approved, you simply add a DivX download link to your Web site and, voila, you're in business. Every time someone clicks on your link to DivX.com and purchases DivX Pro, you'll earn a percentage of the sale.* Read More About the DivX Pro Affiliate Program on DivX.com: %------------------ADVERTISEMENT------------------% PRE-ORDER THE LORD OF THE RINGS: THE FELLOWSHIP OF THE RING Pre-order The Lord of the Rings DVD. Two hours of extra content and a ten-minute behind the scenes look at The Two Towers. Click to buy! %------------------ADVERTISEMENT------------------% Japanese Crime Noir in DivX Video --------------------------------- A wide variety of innovative studios and film distributors have been using DivX video-on-demand technology to sell downloadable DivX titles online since August of last year. One of the many interesting films currently available is the Japanese crime thriller ""Nobody"" from DivX partner Vanguard Cinema. Called ""a triumph"" by the New York Times, ""Nobody"" is an action-packed thriller-noir that tells a story of an escalating cycle of violence and revenge. In the tradition of international crime thrillers like ""La Femme Nikita"" and ""Tokyo Fist,"" ""Nobody"" takes the viewer through a labyrinth of suspense and carnage without release until the very end.* Watch ""Nobody"" in High-quality DivX Video: Mad Phat Crazy DivX Player Skins --------------------------------- The week's dopest DivX Player Skins: * DivX 4 Real: Puts an interesting DivX spin on a familiar player design--keeping it real. Submitted by ""DIVX Budman."" * More DivX: Who doesn't want more DivX? A very colorful, very circular design submitted by one of our old favorite skin authors: ""JC."" * Luxury: Live the good life with this high-class, luxurious gold skin. Submitted by ""scoj."" For all the new skins, check out the DivX Skins section: . If you'd like to make your own skin, read the ""How to Create a Skin"" page: . That's all we have for you this week. Thanks for supporting DivX video. Please feel free to send us any comments and suggestions at . Until next time, The DivX Team P.S. Kelis’ milkshakes brought HIM to the yard?!,"Netflix restaurants, media monopolies and more. ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ The Culture Catchall Welcome to The Culture Catchall, where we discuss weekly pop culture moments that may have flown under your radar but that I’ve conveniently rounded up here. I’m your host, Ruth Etiesit Samuel, a culture reporter at HuffPost.After narrowly escaping a sepia-colored climate crisis, I’m back baby! And so is the 10th season of my personal guilty pleasure series “Love Island U.K.” (I’m several episodes behind, so no spoilers, please!)But let’s not overlook the chaotic end to Netflix’s “The Ultimatum: Queer Love,” with one final liveblog brought to you by Taryn Finley and Erin E. Evans. Whether you love or hate this format, get ready for more, because starting June 26, I and other members of the newsroom will be keeping up with Season 20 of ABC's “The Bachelorette.” Grab your popcorn and wine, and follow along with me on Monday nights. As for the current state of television, the WGA writers strike is still going on. Tony Awards host Ariana DeBose broke down how the strike leaves show presenters without the witty banter, jokes and teleprompters that make operations run smoothly. (Get into HuffPost’s coverage of the historic night here.) While the Directors Guild of America seemingly reached a tentative deal with the AMPTP, the WGA and SAG-AFTRA are standing their ground. By an “overwhelming margin,” 98% of the SAG-AFTRA members who voted have approved a strike if they can’t reach a deal with studios by June 30. I smell a double strike on the way…Speaking of networks, the BET Awards are right around the corner, airing Sunday, June 25, at 8 p.m. ET/PT. Though a host has yet to be announced, the show will be broadcast live from Microsoft Theater in Los Angeles. Certified lover boy and cornball Drake leads with seven award nominations, followed by Memphis rapper GloRilla with six. (This also means that we’re approaching the one-year anniversary of The Culture Catchall, woo!) Amid all the fanfare, Tyler Perry is rumored to have won the bid to buy BET, but rumors are just that — the news has not been confirmed.Glad to be back in the driver’s seat. Buckle up, folks, because we’ve got a lot of ground to cover. Here's the latest edition of The Culture Catchall. Read more We're Still Talking About It “Abbott Elementary” star Tyler James Williams is not playing into y’all’s latent queerphobia this Pride Month, OK?! Clearing up rumors about his sexuality, Williams talked about the dangers of overanalyzing someone’s actions, mannerisms, etc. and tethering it to their sexuality. After Williams shared his thoughts via some now-expired Instagram stories, his little brother chimed in, applauding him. HuffPost’s Jazmin Tolliver has all the heartwarming deets. The Recording Academy has seemingly heard our cries. The Grammy Awards will be undergoing some reform with the addition of three new categories: Best African Music Performance, Best Pop Dance Recording and Best Alternative Jazz Album. (Remember my gripe about an Afrobeats category? Let’s go ahead and add clairvoyance to my resume.) “African music” is a bit too broad for my liking, but this is a start, nonetheless. After the past few tumultuous years, the Hollywood Foreign Press Association will shut down — and be acquired by Dick Clark Productions and Todd Boehly’s investment company Eldridge. What does this mean? The Golden Globes will now be owned by Penske Media Corporation, which also owns Variety, Deadline, The Hollywood Reporter, Billboard, Rolling Stone and many other outlets. Is this a monopoly, or a pending antitrust lawsuit? Only time will tell. Well, Trump isn’t the only one facing time in the pen, chile. Tory Lanez’s sentencing has been delayed yet again
column2: this time, the judge pushed the date to Aug. 7. The Canadian rapper faces up to 22 years in prison for shooting Megan Thee Stallion in July 2020. According to Meghann Thee Reporter, his lawyers’ sentencing recommendation is due Aug. 1. Prosecutors have asked that Lanez be sentenced to 13 years in a state prison instead. The British royal family is back in the news. Prince Harry finally testified in court as a part of a phone-hacking lawsuit he leveraged against the tabloids. According to The Cut, four years ago, Diana’s younger son sued Mirror Group Newspapers for “allegedly hacking his phone and misusing private information to create salacious stories.” The “Spare” author’s comments on the stand and more can be found here. Read More Industry News & Announcements Janelle Monáe graces the cover of British Vogue’s Pride issue. Ke$ha won a years-long defamation case leveraged by producer Dr. Luke. Netflix is now embarking on opening restaurants, instead of lowering subscription prices. Alex Newell and J. Harrison Ghee made history as the first openly nonbinary actors to win Tonys. Track Olympian Tori Bowie died in May due to childbirth complications, per an autopsy. Kelis and Bill Murray are rumored to be dating. (Yes, you read that right.) Taylor Swift and “The 1975” frontman Matty Healy have reportedly broken up. NBC’s “Grand Crew” has been canceled after two seasons on the network. “I Love That For You” has been canceled by Showtime after one season. “All American: Homecoming” has been renewed for a third season at The CW. Netflix’s “They Cloned Tyrone,” starring Teyonah Parris, Jamie Foxx and John Boyega, drops July 21. For the first time in 30 years, no hip-hop album or song has hit No. 1 in 2023. “Black Panther” star Tenoch Huerta is facing accusations of abusive and sexually predatory behavior. Drew Barrymore lands the cover of New York magazine’s television issue. CNN CEO Chris Licht will be leaving the company after a startlingly brief time in the role, per Puck News. Insider News employees have now been on strike for 12 days after “steep layoffs.” Lindsay Lohan talks Dubai, pregnancy and her next chapter in the June cover story of Allure. Prime Video’s “The Summer I Turned Pretty” returns for Season 2 on July 14. “Spider-Man” star Shameik Moore is the cover star of Ebony’s June issue. Wade Goodwyn, longtime NPR newscaster, has died at the age of 63. Neil Everett, longtime “SportsCenter” anchor, is leaving ESPN after 23 years. “Vanderpump Rules” star Ariana Madix covers Glamour’s June issue. “Bling Empire” star Anna Shay, 62, died from a stroke on June 5, per People. Spotify is laying off 200 employees as part of “restructuring” its podcast division. Olivia Rodrigo plans to release “Vampire,” her first single since “Sour,” on June 30. Bronx rapper Ice Spice is Teen Vogue’s June cover star. Doja Cat finally reveals new music coming June 16, per Twitter. Edward Enninful will step away from British Vogue’s editor-in-chief position into a “global advisory role.” Padma Lakshmi is leaving “Top Chef” after 17 years as a host and producer on the show. It’s official: Tennis star Naomi Osaka is having a baby girl with rapper boyfriend Cordae. “Sex Lives of College Girls” star Renee Rapp announces new album “Snow Angel,” dropping Aug. 18. The Los Angeles Times has cut 74 newsroom positions due to “advertising declines.” “Bob's Burgers” star Jay Johnston has been charged by the DOJ for allegedly storming the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6. ""Bottoms,” starring Ayo Edebiri and Rachel Sennott, premieres in theaters on Aug. 25. From The Desk HuffPost’s Culture Desk has been up to a lot in the past few weeks. Here are some of the most recent happenings from my colleagues and me: Senior culture reporter Marina Fang reviewed the A24 film “Past Lives,” written and directed by the critically acclaimed playwright Celine Song. Starring Greta Lee, the romantic drama follows two former childhood friends who reconnect decades later for one week. Fang wrote about how the film’s comparisons to Richard Linklater’s “Before” trilogy don't quite do it justice — and why “Past Lives” will surprise you at every turn. Beware of minor spoilers ahead. Senior culture reporter Taryn Finley talked to hip-hop pioneer Yo-Yo for the latest installment of “I Run This,” a series spotlighting Black women and femmes making waves in culture and entertainment. Finley chatted with the Los Angeles-born rapper and actor about being Ice Cube’s protégée, reflecting on the 50th anniversary of hip-hop, and embarking on a new chapter as she launches her own cooking show. Dig in here, pun intended. Closing out Asian Pacific American Heritage Month, I profiled 20-year-old Aoki Lee Simmons, daughter of supermodel Kimora Lee Simmons and entertainment mogul Russell Simmons. Aoki, one of the youngest graduates in Harvard’s history, spoke to me about her plans after undergrad, navigating race and identity, and walking in her mother’s footsteps — literally. Get into it. As “Transformers: Rise of the Beasts” takes off in theaters, Black Voices editor Finley talked to actor Dominique Fishback about her remarkable year. The “Swarm” star talked to Finley about her East New York roots, her ability to fully immerse herself in different roles, and her goal to revolutionize film and acting. Learn more about Fishback’s burgeoning career here. Senior culture reporter Fang wrote about “Succession” creator Jesse Armstrong and his statement of solidarity in support of the writers strike. In an interview with NPR’s “Fresh Air with Terry Gross,” Armstrong explained how writers are striking over the very conditions that make shows like ""Succession"" possible. Read up here. Inadequate audience targeting and insufficient content might be contributing factors. We have analyzed your campaign metrics and adjusted social media posting times, which has led to noticeable improvements. To further identify the root cause and implement effective solutions, a detailed analysis is needed. Could you please provide a convenient time for a call to discuss these issues in more detail? Urgent Improvement of Data Encryption and Access Controls,"Respected Customer Support, I am contacting you to highlight my concerns regarding the current data encryption and access controls in place for medical records and the hospital's IT systems. Given the critical nature of patient data security, which can be compromised with severe repercussions, I strongly advocate for the swift enhancement of these security measures. This includes the adoption of strong encryption methods, the use of multi-factor authentication, and the implementation of stringent access controls. Moreover, it is imperative to perform regular security evaluations and penetration tests to detect and rectify any potential weaknesses. Could you please furnish me with details on the initiatives currently underway to bolster data encryption and access controls? I am eager to receive your feedback on the steps taken. I am looking forward to your response. Thank you for your swift action towards securing patient information.",0,1
Re: URGENT: Mercury Language Integration with IAM Permissions Issue,"Dear John,
Thank you for contacting Aetheros Support. We understand the urgency of this issue and are here to help.
To assist us in diagnosing the problem, could you please provide the following information:
* The specific version of the Mercury Language SDK you are using.
* A code snippet demonstrating how you are integrating Mercury with the IAM service.
* The relevant IAM role configurations and permissions.
Once we have this information, we can investigate further and provide a solution.
In the meantime, you can refer to our documentation on Mercury Language and IAM integration here: [link to documentation]
We will keep you updated on the progress.

We have received your message concerning a potential data breach that you believe may have resulted from outdated Cisco IOS. We understand the importance of securing your patient records and are committed to addressing this matter promptly. For a more comprehensive analysis and to offer a suitable resolution, could you please share any error messages you encountered while attempting to update the system, along with the current version of Cisco IOS you are using? Also, could you confirm your account number <acc_num> so I can investigate further? If additional details are needed, I might arrange a call at a convenient time for you.
